description: §52.15.5/§52.8 — a per-user row-scoped Pattern-C cell (`SELECT * ... WHERE user_id = ${@currentUser.id}`) fires no I-SSR-AUTH-SCOPED-CLIENT-HYDRATED: it is NOT auto-omitted from the SSR seed. The compose handler runs it UNDER the request, so each viewer's first paint contains only their own rows (anon → @currentUser.id is not → SQL NULL → zero rows, fail-closed) — no cross-user leak. The clean control for the auto-make-safe omission: row-scoped cells are seeded, not omitted.
rationale: §52.15.5: the `${@currentUser.id}` WHERE clause row-scopes the query to the request user (a LIVE server-ambient interpolation counted by `queryInterpolationsAreServerAmbientOnly`), so the SSR pre-render is per-user and the cell stays SSR-seeded — I-SSR-AUTH-SCOPED-CLIENT-HYDRATED does not fire (§52.15.3). The auto-omission never over-omits a row-scoped cell.
